hypothesis: Incorporating reaction pathway and synthesis condition data from chemical literature into the pre-training corpus—alongside MOF structural descriptions and PubChem compound data—will enable LLMs to predict not only intrinsic MOF properties but also synthesizability scores, such that models pre-trained on synthesis-enriched corpora will achieve lower mean absolute error on property prediction tasks with fewer than 5,000 supervised fine-tuning examples compared to models pre-trained solely on structural and abstract-level chemical data, because exposure to mechanistic synthesis language provides implicit constraints on physically realizable MOF configurations that reduce the hypothesis space during fine-tuning.
